title: Implement SQLite database layer with sqlx
description: In crates/teamserver: database module using sqlx with SQLite. Write migrations for all tables: ts_agents (full schema from Havoc), ts_listeners, ts_links (pivot relationships), ts_loot, ts_audit_log (new). Implement repository structs with async CRUD methods for each table. Use sqlx::migrate! macro. Connection pool setup. Write integration tests.
close_reason: Implemented SQLite sqlx database layer, migrations, repositories, and integration tests

title: Embed Python runtime in teamserver via PyO3
description: In crates/teamserver: embed a Python 3.x interpreter using PyO3. Initialize the Python VM at startup. Load .py module files from a configured plugins directory. Expose a Python API module (import red_cell) with bindings for: agent access (get_agent, list_agents), listener access, event subscription (on_agent_checkin, on_command_output), and command registration. Thread-safe GIL management with tokio.
close_reason: Embedded a PyO3 runtime in teamserver startup, added plugin loading from Teamserver.PluginsDir, and exposed the initial red_cell Python API with agent/listener access plus event and command registration hooks.

title: parse_hour_minute validation off-by-one accepts invalid times 24:xx and xx:60
description: In crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs, the parse_hour_minute function at line 1066 checks 'if hour > 24 || minute > 60'. This is off-by-one: hour 24 is not a valid 24-hour clock hour (valid range is 0–23), and minute 60 is not a valid minute (valid range is 0–59). An operator submitting WorkingHours '24:60-00:01' passes validation and gets packed into the agent config. Correct fix: change the guard to 'if hour > 23 || minute > 59'.

title: Teamserver - Demon Agent Protocol
description: Full implementation of the Demon binary C2 protocol for agent communication. Must maintain backward compatibility with existing compiled Demon agents: 0xDEADBEEF magic, AES-256-CBC per-agent encryption, binary packet format, all command IDs (DEMON_INIT=99, COMMAND_CHECKIN=100, COMMAND_GET_JOB=1, etc). Agent cannot be modified.

title: parse_dns_query does not reject DNS response packets (QR bit unchecked)
DnsListenerState::responses map leaks memory — completed downloads never removed
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_download returns the string 'done' when seq >= total (i.e. the agent has polled past the last chunk), but it never removes the corresponding entry from self.responses. The responses HashMap therefore accumulates one entry per agent per checkin cycle and is never cleaned up while the listener is running.

Unlike self.uploads (which is pruned by a retain call on every new upload chunk), self.responses has no expiry or cleanup path. In an active engagement with many agents checking in frequently, this is an unbounded memory leak.

Fix: remove the agent's entry from self.responses once 'done' is returned. Since handle_download takes a shared read lock, the removal should either:
  (a) upgrade to a write lock when idx >= total, remove the entry, and return 'done', or
  (b) change the data structure so that entries are removed from the write side (handle_upload) after a timeout, similar to how uploads are expired.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_download, around line 1453.

title: Implement RBAC system (roles and permissions)
description: In crates/teamserver: role-based access control. Define roles: Admin (full access), Operator (can task agents, manage listeners), Analyst (read-only: view agents, sessions, loot; no tasking). Roles configurable per operator in the profile. Middleware/extractor that checks permissions on each WebSocket command and REST endpoint. Write unit tests covering permission boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented operator roles, RBAC extractors, websocket authorization, and boundary tests

title: REST API handler tests do not cover update, delete, start, stop, or mark endpoints
description: The test module in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs covers: GET / (root), GET /listeners (auth required, role check, rate limit), GET /openapi.json, and 404 fallback. Missing coverage: PUT /listeners/{name} (update), DELETE /listeners/{name}, POST /listeners/{name}/start, POST /listeners/{name}/stop, and POST /listeners/{name}/mark. Each of these has distinct success/error paths (e.g. 404 not-found, 409 already-running, 422 start failure). Add unit tests for each handler exercising at least the happy path and the most important error case.

title: plugins: invoke_registered_command broadcasts AgentTask with unrelated task_id
description: In plugins.rs::invoke_registered_command (line 517), the operator broadcast AgentTask message is given a new task_id via next_request_id(). However, the actual queued job (enqueued during the Python callback via agent.task()) was already assigned task_id and request_id from earlier next_request_id() calls inside queue_raw_agent_task. The broadcast task_id is a completely different counter value and will never match the queued job's task_id, breaking operator-side task tracking and output correlation. The fix is to capture the task_id at enqueue time and thread it through to the broadcast.

title: Unused import DemonCallback in dispatch.rs produces compiler warning
description:
dispatch.rs imports DemonCallback from red_cell_common::demon but does not use it anywhere. This generates a compiler warning. Either use it in the callback handler implementations (likely the correct fix once handle_filesystem_callback and handle_beacon_output_callback are implemented to dispatch on DemonCallback variants), or remove the import.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:13

title: plugins: emit_agent_checkin and emit_command_output lack unit tests
description: The two public async methods PluginRuntime::emit_agent_checkin() and PluginRuntime::emit_command_output() in crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs (lines 146 and 155) have no unit tests. These are the core event-emission entry points called by dispatch.rs; they need tests that: (1) verify callbacks registered via on_agent_checkin/on_command_output are actually invoked when emitted, and (2) verify errors are propagated correctly when a callback raises a Python exception.

title: plugins: large WIP in git stash is not committed — work may be lost
description: git stash list shows stash@{0} on main at 75da201 containing ~536 net changes to crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs, crates/teamserver/src/main.rs, and crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs. This is significant in-progress work for red-cell-c2-25h (Python API bindings) that was never committed. The stash was likely left accidentally after a QA pull. The dev agent should: (1) apply the stash, (2) review the diff, (3) commit or integrate the changes. If left as-is the work risks being dropped.
close_reason: Recovered orphaned plugin stash by archiving it to branch recovered/red-cell-c2-25h-stash and confirming its work landed in f903f5c

title: request_id silently defaults to 0 when task_id is not valid hex
description: In crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs line 599: u32::from_str_radix(info.task_id.trim(), 16).unwrap_or_default() — if the operator sends a task_id that is not a valid hex string (empty, malformed, or decimal), request_id silently becomes 0. This means correlation between operator task and agent response is lost without any warning. Fix: return an error or log a warning when task_id cannot be parsed, rather than silently defaulting to 0.

title: handle_process_command_callback errors on Modules, Grep, Memory subcommands
description: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs handle_process_command_callback (registered for COMMAND_PROC) only handles DemonProcessCommand::Create (=4) and DemonProcessCommand::Kill (=7). The other defined subcommands — Modules=2, Grep=3, Memory=6 — fall into the 'other' arm and return CommandDispatchError::InvalidCallbackPayload. If the Demon agent sends any of these callbacks, the response is dropped and the operator sees nothing. At minimum log and emit a generic operator event, or implement the full handlers.

title: Implement Python API bindings for teamserver
description: In crates/teamserver: full Python API surface. RegisterCommand(name, description, callback): adds custom command visible to operators. RegisterCallback(event_type, callback): calls Python on agent events. Agent object: .task(command, args), .info (hostname, user, etc). Listener object: .start(), .stop(). Compatible with existing Havoc Python module conventions where possible. Write example module and tests.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible teamserver Python bindings, plugin command execution, listener controls, example module, and tests

title: add_bytes silently emits zero-length prefix on slice > 4 GiB instead of returning error
description: In crates/teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs at line 1088, the helper 'add_bytes' calls 'u32::try_from(value.len()).unwrap_or_default()'. If the slice ever exceeds u32::MAX (~4 GiB), the length prefix is silently written as 0, corrupting the packed config binary stream rather than propagating an error. Should propagate a PayloadBuildError::InvalidRequest instead. In practice payloads are never this large, but the silent truncation is an incorrect invariant: the code should panic or return Err, never silently proceed with corrupt data.

title: build_dns_txt_response hardcodes TXT QTYPE in question section regardless of original query type
description:
In listeners.rs, build_dns_txt_response reconstructs the question section of the DNS response by writing qname_raw followed by DNS_TYPE_TXT (16) and DNS_CLASS_IN. However, the original query's QTYPE is not passed in, so if the query was an A record query (QTYPE=1), the response question section claims QTYPE=TXT (16), which violates RFC 1035 §4.1.2 (the question section in a response must echo the original question).

The ParsedDnsQuery struct already contains a qtype field. The fix is to add a qtype parameter to build_dns_txt_response (or pass the full ParsedDnsQuery) and use query.qtype instead of DNS_TYPE_TXT when writing the question section.

Priority 3 — this is a protocol correctness issue but doesn't affect C2 functionality since all C2 traffic uses TXT queries, and the current code always responds with a TXT answer regardless.

title: handle_token_callback returns error for all subcommands except Impersonate
description: crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs handle_token_callback (registered for COMMAND_TOKEN) only handles DemonTokenCommand::Impersonate and propagates a CommandDispatchError for every other subcommand (Steal=2, List=3, PrivsGetOrList=4, Make=5, GetUid=6, Revert=7, Remove=8, Clear=9, FindTokens=10). When the Demon agent sends back a callback for any of those subcommands, the handler fails, causing the response to be silently dropped (the error is logged but no operator event is emitted). Either implement the remaining subcommands or handle them with a generic/no-op response rather than returning an error that kills the callback.

title: DNS listener response map (DnsPendingResponse) has no expiry, causing unbounded memory growth
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState contains a 'responses: Mutex<HashMap<u32, DnsPendingResponse>>' that is populated when an agent upload is processed successfully. Entries are never removed: handle_download returns 'done' when the agent has polled all chunks but does not call responses.remove(&agent_id). Under active C2 use, every completed agent interaction leaves a stale entry in this map.

By contrast, the uploads map does expire entries via 'uploads.retain(|_, p| p.received_at.elapsed().as_secs() < DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS)' called in try_assemble_upload.

Fix: either (a) remove the entry from responses inside handle_download when idx >= total (converting &self to &mut self or using interior mutability properly), or (b) add a similar time-based expiry by adding a received_at timestamp to DnsPendingResponse and purging in handle_download or a background cleanup task.

title: Typo "impersonat" in token callback error message
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs line 479, the error message reads "Failed to impersonat {user}" — missing the trailing "e" in "impersonate". This is displayed verbatim in the operator console.

Fix: change to "Failed to impersonate {user}".

title: Build broken: 3 filesystem callback handlers called but not defined
description:
dispatch.rs calls handle_filesystem_callback, handle_command_output_callback, and handle_beacon_output_callback in two places each, but none of these functions are defined anywhere in the codebase. This causes 6 compile errors and prevents the workspace from building.

Affected locations:
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:172 (handle_filesystem_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:205 (handle_command_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:220 (handle_beacon_output_callback in handler closure)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:611 (handle_filesystem_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:621 (handle_command_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)
- crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:624 (handle_beacon_output_callback in route_callback_by_command)

These must be implemented as async fns in dispatch.rs to fix the build. The filesystem callback should parse DemonCallback variants (CALLBACK_FILE for chunked download reassembly, CALLBACK_FILE_NOTFOUND, CALLBACK_OUTPUT, etc.) and broadcast results to operators. See the DemonCallback enum in common for the expected message types.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: plugins: queue_raw_agent_task generates mismatched request_id and task_id
description: In plugins.rs::queue_raw_agent_task (line 468/471), request_id and task_id are generated by two separate calls to next_request_id(), so they always have different counter values (e.g. request_id=5, task_id=6). task_id should be derived from the same counter value as request_id — e.g. let id = next_request_id(); then use id for request_id and format!("{:08X}", id) for task_id. As-is, any system that correlates a queued job by its task_id will fail.

title: parse_api_agent_id uses decimal for all-digit agent IDs, breaking hex-only IDs like 00000010
Fix clippy too-many-arguments violation in sockets.rs register_client
description: cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings fails with: error: this function has too many arguments (9/8) at crates/teamserver/src/sockets.rs:329 in the register_client method. This breaks CI clippy gates. Fix: refactor arguments into a struct (e.g. ClientRegistration) or use #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] with justification.
close_reason: register_client was refactored to accept a PendingClient struct (3 params), clippy now passes clean

title: audit: query_audit_log loads entire table into memory before filtering
description:
crates/teamserver/src/audit.rs:187-208 — query_audit_log calls database.audit_log().list().await? which fetches all rows from the database, converts every row to AuditRecord, reverses the entire Vec, then applies in-memory filtering and slice-based pagination.

For a busy teamserver with thousands of agent tasks logged per day, this will:
- Consume unbounded memory proportional to total audit log size
- Increase latency linearly with log size even for small page requests
- Convert and deserialize all rows even when only 1 page of 50 is needed

Fix: push filtering and pagination into SQL. The audit_log repository should expose a filtered query method that accepts WHERE clauses for actor, action, target_kind, target_id, and a JSON-extracted agent_id/command/result_status, plus LIMIT and OFFSET. COUNT(*) with the same WHERE can provide the total without fetching all rows.

title: handle_pivot_callback has 9 args — fails clippy too_many_arguments
description:
crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:438 — handle_pivot_callback now takes 9 parameters after the PluginRuntime argument was added (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins, agent_id, request_id, payload). Clippy's too_many_arguments lint fires (limit is 8) and -D warnings promotes it to an error, breaking 'cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings'.

Fix: introduce a DispatchContext struct (or similar) to bundle the shared state refs (registry, events, database, sockets, downloads, plugins) and pass it as a single argument, reducing the parameter count below the limit.
close_reason: Resolved while implementing the embedded PyO3 runtime: plugin tests were stabilized and pivot dispatch now uses a shared context, so cargo test and cargo clippy both pass again.

title: build_job silently maps invalid task_id to 0 on parse failure
description: In crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs line 600, `u32::from_str_radix(info.task_id.trim(), 16).unwrap_or_default()` silently maps any malformed or non-hex task_id string to request_id=0. If a client sends a job with an invalid task_id, the resulting callback is stored under task 0, making it unroutable and invisible to the operator. This should return an `AgentCommandError::InvalidNumericField` (the same error path used for other numeric fields like `parse_hex_u32` at line 1152) rather than silently defaulting.

title: DNS listener silently drops non-C2-domain queries instead of responding with REFUSED
description:
In listeners.rs, DnsListenerState::handle_dns_packet uses the ? operator on parse_dns_c2_query, which returns None when labels don't match the configured C2 domain. This causes the whole function to return None, so no UDP response is sent to the client. The failing test (dns_listener_starts_and_responds_to_unknown_queries_with_refused) demonstrates this: it sends a query for 'something.other.domain.com' to a listener configured for 'c2.example.com' and expects a REFUSED (RCODE=5) response, but times out instead.

Fix: replace 'let c2_query = parse_dns_c2_query(&query.labels, &self.config.domain)?;' with explicit None handling that falls through to 'Some(build_dns_refused_response(query.id))' when the domain doesn't match.

This is a P1 bug because it causes a test failure and means non-C2 DNS traffic going to the listener is silently dropped instead of being refused, which can confuse legitimate DNS resolvers expecting a response.
close_reason: Returned REFUSED for non-matching DNS queries instead of dropping the packet silently

title: Client - UI Components
description: All egui UI panels and widgets: login dialog, main window layout, session table (agent list), session graph (node visualization), command console (interactive per-agent shell), file browser, process list, loot viewer, and multi-operator chat panel.

title: DownloadTracker created fresh per route call — download state never persists
description:
In route_callback_by_command (dispatch.rs:603), a new DownloadTracker::default() is created on every call. This means any multi-chunk download reassembly state stored in the tracker would be immediately discarded at the end of each invocation — chunks would never accumulate into a complete file.

The DownloadTracker should be:
1. Passed in as a parameter from the shared instance in CommandDispatcher::with_handlers (line 131), OR
2. Stored on the shared state struct that route_callback_by_command is called from.

Using a per-call fresh tracker defeats the entire purpose of the HashMap<(u32, u32), DownloadState>. Note that route_callback_by_command at line 603 appears to be a legacy dispatch path; the handler-registration approach in with_handlers (line 131) uses the shared downloads instance. If route_callback_by_command is still active, it must share the same DownloadTracker.
close_reason: Implemented the missing filesystem callback handlers and fixed shared download tracker reuse in builtin dispatch

title: encode_pivot_job_payload and write_len_prefixed_bytes silently corrupt packets on oversized input
description:
Three length-prefix writes use `u32::try_from(len).unwrap_or_default()`, which silently emits 0 instead of an error when the slice length exceeds u32::MAX (4 GiB):
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:544 (payload.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:552 (inner.len() in encode_pivot_job_payload)
- crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:1170 (write_len_prefixed_bytes)

If this code path is ever reached with an oversized buffer, the agent receives a length field of 0 and misreads the framing of all subsequent fields, silently corrupting the protocol stream. These helpers should return a Result and propagate an error rather than defaulting to 0.

title: Reconcile Demon transport crypto mode with Havoc CTR reference
description: Issue red-cell-c2-499 requested AES-256-CBC helpers in crates/common, but the Havoc reference implementation currently uses AES-CTR for Demon traffic on both the teamserver and agent sides (see src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/common/crypt/aes.go and src/Havoc/payloads/Demon/src/crypt/AesCrypt.c). Before wiring crypto into packet parsing and handshake code, verify the intended on-wire mode for Red Cell and either switch the implementation or document an intentional protocol divergence.
close_reason: Verified Havoc uses AES-CTR, switched shared agent crypto helpers to CTR, and corrected protocol docs

title: Client - Python API
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the client for operator scripting. Python script manager UI panel, custom command registration from scripts, event callbacks, and access to agent/listener objects from Python. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc client-side Python modules.

title: "Erro" truncated event type in kerberos and socket callback handlers
description:
In crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs, 8 error events use "Erro" instead of "Error" as the Type field sent to operator clients. The Havoc operator client relies on this string for color-coding and display formatting — "Error" (red), "Good" (green), "Info" (blue). Using "Erro" means error conditions are silently shown with no color coding.

Affected lines in dispatch.rs: 610, 635, 657, 673, 721, 772, 793, 815

Handlers affected: handle_kerberos_callback (Luid, Klist, Purge, Ptt subcommands) and handle_socket_callback (ReversePortForward start/clear, Read, Write failure branches).

Fix: replace all instances of "Erro" with "Error" in agent_response_event kind arguments in these handlers.

title: audit: update_listener and delete_listener REST endpoints missing audit trail
description:
The audit trail implementation in 001245d covers listener.create, listener.start, and listener.stop for both the REST API and WebSocket paths, but update_listener (PUT /api/v1/listeners/{name}) and delete_listener (DELETE /api/v1/listeners/{name}) are not audited.

Both handlers still use _identity (underscore-prefixed, identity ignored) and contain no record_audit_entry calls. A listener deletion is a security-relevant event that must appear in the audit log.

Affected files:
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:929-941 (update_listener) 
- crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:958-965 (delete_listener)

Fix: change _identity to identity in both handlers and add record_audit_entry calls for success and failure paths, consistent with the pattern already used by create_listener / start_listener / stop_listener.

title: HTTP 429 rate-limit response missing Retry-After header
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs, when the rate limit is exceeded the ApiAuthError::RateLimited variant serialises retry_after_seconds into the JSON response body, but the HTTP Retry-After header (RFC 9110 §10.2.3) is never set on the response. HTTP clients, proxies, and rate-limit-aware libraries look for this header and will not back off correctly without it. Fix: in the IntoResponse implementation for ApiAuthError::RateLimited, add the Retry-After header to the response with value retry_after_seconds before returning the JSON body.

title: plugins: silent mutex poison recovery in install_as_active/active() can mask panics
description: In crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs lines 171-188, both install_as_active() and active() recover a poisoned Mutex by calling poisoned.into_inner(). A poisoned mutex means another thread panicked while holding the lock, leaving the guarded value in an unknown state. Silently recovering it means the plugin runtime could be left in a corrupted state with no indication. The correct approach is to treat a poisoned lock as a fatal error (return Err or panic with a clear message), not silently ignore it. This could cause subtle, hard-to-debug correctness issues.

title: websocket: execute_agent_task has triplicated job-queuing block
description: In websocket.rs::execute_agent_task (lines 633-654), the three branches (plugin command returns false, plugin present but no command matched, no plugin runtime) each contain an identical 5-line block: build_jobs -> queue all -> return count. This should be extracted into a shared path to avoid divergence. If one branch is fixed (e.g. error handling change) the others are silently missed.

title: read_smb_frame allocates unbounded heap buffer from untrusted length field
description:
In listeners.rs, read_smb_frame (line 1173) reads a 32-bit length field directly from the SMB pipe stream and immediately allocates 'vec![0_u8; payload_len]' without any upper-bound check. A malicious or malfunctioning client can send a length of up to u32::MAX (~4 GB), triggering a multi-gigabyte heap allocation that will likely panic or OOM-kill the teamserver process.

Fix: add a sanity cap before the allocation. A reasonable limit is the maximum Demon packet size (e.g. 16 MiB or whatever is used for the HTTP path). Return an io::Error(InvalidData) if payload_len exceeds that cap, which will cause handle_smb_connection to log a warning and close the connection cleanly.

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, read_smb_frame function, line ~1184-1187.
close_reason: Rejected oversized SMB frame lengths before allocation and added regression coverage.

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (agent tasking)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands for agent interaction. AgentTask: validate agent exists, check operator RBAC permission, enqueue job in agent's job queue, broadcast AgentTask event to all operators. AgentKill. AgentNote (add note to agent). AgentRemove. All with proper RBAC checks.
close_reason: Implemented operator WebSocket handlers for agent task, note, kill, and remove commands with RBAC checks and registry updates

title: Teamserver - Authentication & RBAC
description: Operator authentication (SHA3-256 password hashing, session management) and role-based access control system. Operators should have differentiated permissions (e.g. admin, operator, analyst/read-only). This is a new feature beyond what Havoc provides.

title: Implement Demon payload builder
description: In crates/teamserver: wrap the Demon agent compilation pipeline in Rust. Given a listener config and Demon options (sleep, jitter, sleep obfuscation technique, injection settings, output format), invoke MinGW and NASM to compile a Demon binary. Stream build output to operator. Deliver compiled EXE/DLL/shellcode bytes back via WebSocket. Validate compiler toolchain availability at startup.
close_reason: Implemented a validated Demon payload builder with WebSocket streaming, artifact delivery, and startup toolchain checks

title: Implement AES-256-CBC crypto for agent communication
description: In crates/common: implement AES-256-CBC encryption/decryption for Demon agent communications. Each agent has a unique 32-byte key and 16-byte IV. Use the aes + cbc crates (RustCrypto). Functions: encrypt_agent_data(key, iv, plaintext) -> Vec<u8>, decrypt_agent_data(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>>. Key generation for new agents. Write unit tests verifying interoperability with known Havoc-produced ciphertext.
close_reason: Implemented AES-256-CBC agent crypto helpers in crates/common with encryption, decryption, key generation, and unit tests.

title: Set up egui client binary and app skeleton
description: In crates/client: main.rs entry point for red-cell-client. eframe/egui app setup with window configuration (title, size, dark theme). App state struct holding: connection status, operator info, list of agents, listeners, loot, chat messages. Placeholder panels for all UI sections. CLI args: --server <url> for teamserver address.

title: Teamserver - REST API
description: HTTP REST API on the teamserver for automation and external tooling integration. OpenAPI spec, endpoints for agents (list/show/kill/interact), listeners (CRUD), loot/credentials, and jobs. Separate authentication from WebSocket (API keys or JWT). New feature beyond Havoc.

title: Implement screenshot, SOCKS5, and Kerberos commands
description: In crates/teamserver: COMMAND_SCREENSHOT (receive screenshot bytes, save to loot, notify operators), COMMAND_SOCKET/SOCKS5 proxy (relay TCP connections through the agent - requires async bidirectional streaming), COMMAND_KERBEROS (roasting, delegation attacks - parse and format results for operators).
close_reason: Implemented screenshot loot persistence, SOCKS5 relay handling, and Kerberos/socket command support

title: Teamserver - Listeners
description: C2 listener implementations: HTTP/HTTPS (profile-configured, custom URIs/headers/user-agents), SMB named pipe listener, and listener lifecycle management (create, start, stop, persist to DB). DNS listener planned as follow-on after HTTP and SMB.

title: Common Crate (shared types, protocol, crypto)
description: Implement the shared Rust crate used by both the teamserver and client. Contains: Demon binary protocol types, AES-256-CBC crypto, JSON WebSocket message types for operator communication, TLS certificate generation, and HCL/YAOTL config parsing. This is a foundational dependency for all other crates.

title: Implement process commands (COMMAND_PROC)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all COMMAND_PROC (0x1010) sub-commands: process list (ps), process kill, process inject, token impersonation for spawn. Parse binary subcommand and parameters. Serialize job for agent. Parse callback output and format for operator display.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible process tasking/callback handling for proc list/create/kill, shellcode inject, and token impersonation.

title: Implement HTTP route for agent callbacks
description: In crates/teamserver: Axum route(s) for Demon agent HTTP callbacks. POST handler on configurable URIs (from listener profile). Read raw body bytes, validate minimum size, check magic bytes before decryption. Return 404 for invalid requests (operational security). Integrate with listener registry so only routes for active listeners accept traffic. Must handle both HTTP and HTTPS.
close_reason: Implemented HTTP(S) Demon callback validation with decoy 404 handling for malformed traffic.

title: Implement HCL/YAOTL config parsing in common crate
description: Parse the existing Havoc .yaotl profile format (HCL-based) in Rust. Use the hcl-rs crate. Define strongly-typed Rust structs for the full profile schema: Teamserver (Host, Port, Build), Operators (username/password pairs), Demon defaults (Sleep, Jitter, Injection settings), Listener configs (HTTP, SMB, External). Write unit tests with the existing havoc.yaotl profile.
close_reason: Implemented typed YAOTL profile parsing in red-cell-common with Havoc fixture tests.

title: Implement HTTP/HTTPS listener (profile-configured)
description: In crates/teamserver: full HTTP/HTTPS C2 listener. Profile-configurable: custom URIs, HTTP headers (e.g. fake server headers), user-agent filtering, fake 404 for non-agent requests, custom response bodies. HTTPS with auto-generated or profile-specified TLS cert. Bind to configurable host:port. Integrate with Axum router. Support multiple simultaneous HTTP listeners on different ports.
close_reason: Implemented Axum-backed HTTP/HTTPS listener runtime with filtering, fake 404 handling, response customization, and TLS bootstrap

title: Implement session table (agent list panel)
description: In crates/client: egui table panel showing all active agents. Columns: ID, hostname, username, domain, IP, PID, process, arch, elevated, OS, sleep/jitter, last checkin. Sortable columns, color coding (elevated = red highlight). Right-click context menu: interact, kill, add note. Double-click to open command console. Filter/search bar.

title: Implement loot viewer and chat panel
description: In crates/client: loot viewer panel (credentials, files, screenshots - filterable by type/agent/time, download button for files). Multi-operator chat panel (message input + scrollable history with operator names and timestamps, broadcasts via WebSocket chat messages).

title: Teamserver - Python Plugin System
description: Embed Python (via PyO3) in the teamserver to support operator-side scripting modules. Expose API bindings: RegisterCommand, RegisterCallback, agent/listener access. Load .py modules from a configured directory. Preserves compatibility with existing Havoc Python modules where possible.

title: Unit tests for common crate
description: Comprehensive unit tests for crates/common. Protocol: round-trip encoding/decoding of all Demon packet types, edge cases (empty data, max size). Crypto: AES-256-CBC encrypt/decrypt, known-answer tests against Havoc-produced ciphertext. JSON WebSocket messages: serialize/deserialize all event types. Config parsing: test against all three existing .yaotl profile files. Target 90%+ coverage.

title: plugins test panics: Tokio runtime shutting down during Python GIL call
description:
crates/teamserver/src/plugins.rs — test initialize_exposes_agent_and_listener_accessors fails with:
  'A Tokio 1.x context was found, but it is being shutdown.'

Root cause: the test uses std::thread::spawn to call Python::with_gil, but the Python API functions (get_agent, get_listener) internally call Handle::block_on() using the Handle captured at PluginRuntime::initialize() time. By the time the OS thread executes the GIL block, the #[tokio::test] runtime may already be shutting down, causing the block_on() to panic.

Fix: replace std::thread::spawn with tokio::task::spawn_blocking (consistent with the production code in PluginRuntime::initialize), or restructure the test so the Tokio runtime is alive for the duration of the Python call.
close_reason: Resolved while implementing the embedded PyO3 runtime: plugin tests were stabilized and pivot dispatch now uses a shared context, so cargo test and cargo clippy both pass again.

title: Implement Demon binary protocol types
description: In crates/common: define all Rust structs and enums for the Demon binary C2 protocol. Packet header: Size(u32), Magic(u32)=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID(u32). All command IDs as enum: DEMON_INIT(99), COMMAND_CHECKIN(100), COMMAND_GET_JOB(1), COMMAND_PROC(0x1010), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL(22), COMMAND_TOKEN(40), COMMAND_KERBEROS(2550), COMMAND_SCREENSHOT(2510), COMMAND_SOCKET(2540), etc. Callback types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT, CALLBACK_ERROR, CALLBACK_FILE. Implement binary serialization/deserialization (byteorder or binrw crate). Write unit tests for round-trip encoding.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible Demon protocol types, enums, serializers, and tests

title: Implement file browser panel
description: In crates/client: file browser UI for navigating agent file system. Tree view with lazy-load (expand directory triggers ls command). File actions: download, upload (file picker dialog), delete. Show file size, timestamps, permissions. Download progress bar. Integration with loot panel (downloaded files appear in loot).

title: Integration tests: mock Demon agent checkin
description: Integration tests that spin up a real teamserver (in-process, ephemeral SQLite) and simulate a Demon agent. Test flow: start teamserver, connect mock agent over HTTP, send DEMON_INIT packet (properly encrypted with test key), verify AgentNew event emitted, send COMMAND_GET_JOB, enqueue a job via WebSocket, verify agent receives job, send CALLBACK_OUTPUT, verify operator receives result.

title: Implement WebSocket client connection to teamserver
description: In crates/client: async WebSocket client using tokio-tungstenite. Connect to wss://host:port/havoc/. Accept self-signed server certs (same as Havoc behavior). Background tokio task for receiving events and updating shared app state (Arc<Mutex<AppState>>). Sending task for queuing outgoing operator commands. Reconnection with exponential backoff. Connection status indicator in UI.

title: Implement operator event broadcast system
description: In crates/teamserver: event broadcasting infrastructure. Use tokio::sync::broadcast channel to push events to all connected operator WebSocket connections. EventBus struct: subscribe() returns a Receiver, broadcast(event) sends to all. Event types from common crate. Handle slow/disconnected operators (drop lagging receivers). Write unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented teamserver event bus with broadcast subscriptions and unit tests

title: Implement JSON protocol handler and event dispatcher
description: In crates/client: deserialize incoming JSON WebSocket events (using common crate types) and dispatch to UI state updates. AgentNew: add to agents list. AgentUpdate: update agent fields. AgentResponse: append output to agent console. ListenerNew/Update: update listener list. Chat: append to chat. LootNew: update loot panel. All state updates trigger egui repaint.

title: Teamserver - Command Dispatch
description: Command routing and dispatch system: receive parsed commands from agents, route to appropriate handlers, serialize responses back to agents. Covers all Demon commands: process ops, file system, token manipulation, injection, shell execution, Kerberos, screenshot, SOCKS5 proxy, SMB pivot management.

title: Implement command dispatch router
description: In crates/teamserver: central command dispatch module. Routes incoming parsed Demon commands (by command ID) to appropriate handler functions. CommandDispatcher struct with a registry of handlers. Each handler receives: agent ID, request ID, raw data bytes; returns serialized response bytes to enqueue. Modular design so new commands can be added easily.
close_reason: Implemented a central Demon command dispatcher with built-in get-job and checkin handlers.

title: Teamserver - Operator WebSocket API
description: WebSocket endpoint for operator clients. Handles connections, authentication handshake, event broadcasting to all connected operators, and message routing (create listener, generate payload, execute commands, etc). Must match the JSON-over-WebSocket protocol compatible with the red-cell-client.

title: Implement operator session synchronization on connect
description: In crates/teamserver: when a new operator connects and authenticates, send them the full current state: all active agents (AgentNew events), all running listeners, recent activity log. This brings a newly connected client up to date without requiring a full reconnect or restart. Match Havoc behavior.
close_reason: Added connect-time operator snapshot replay for running listeners, recent logs, and active agents

title: Implement SMB named pipe listener
description: In crates/teamserver: SMB pivot listener using Windows named pipes. Listen on a configurable named pipe path. Receive Demon binary packets from parent agents acting as SMB pivots. Parse and process identically to HTTP packets. Must run on Linux (use a cross-platform named pipe abstraction or document Linux testing limitations). Persist listener config to DB.
close_reason: Implemented a cross-platform SMB listener runtime with shared Demon packet handling and SMB transport tests

title: E2E tests: mock agent + mock operator client
description: End-to-end tests simulating a full operator session. Spin up teamserver, connect mock operator WebSocket client, authenticate, verify session sync, create a listener, connect mock Demon agent, verify agent appears in operator's session table event, task the agent, verify job delivery and response round-trip. Run in CI on every PR.

title: Implement in-memory agent registry
description: In crates/teamserver: thread-safe in-memory agent registry using DashMap or tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap>. Supports: insert new agent, get agent by ID, list all active agents, update agent fields, mark agent as dead, get/set per-agent AES key+IV, per-agent job queue (VecDeque<Job> behind a Mutex). Sync with DB on changes. Write unit tests for concurrency.
close_reason: Implemented a DB-backed in-memory agent registry with AES state, job queues, and concurrency tests

title: Implement Demon packet parser and AES handshake
description: In crates/teamserver: parse incoming Demon binary packets. Read 12-byte header (Size, Magic=0xDEADBEEF, AgentID). For new agents (DEMON_INIT): handle key exchange to establish per-agent AES-256-CBC key+IV, store in agent registry. For existing agents: look up key by AgentID, decrypt payload, return parsed command ID + request ID + data bytes. All using types from common crate.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible Demon packet parsing, init registration, and callback decryption.

title: Workspace fails to compile: api.rs references items only in git stash
description:
The working tree has `crates/teamserver/src/api.rs` (agent REST endpoints) that references:
- `AgentRegistry::list()` — method only in git stash (not committed, not in working tree)
- `crate::websocket::execute_agent_task` — function not yet pub(crate) in committed code
- `AgentCommandError` — enum not yet pub(crate) in committed code
- `ToSchema` impls for `AgentInfo`, `AgentEncryptionInfo`, `AgentTaskInfo` — derives only in stash

Running `cargo check --workspace` produces 21 compiler errors. The stash contains the prerequisite changes but has not been applied.

Fix: apply the stash (`git stash pop`) before committing the api.rs additions. All pieces need to land in the same commit (or the stash changes committed first).
close_reason: Resolved by commits ebfbbfe and dc4ae40 — work was committed and build passes cleanly.

title: Implement operator audit trail
description: In crates/teamserver: persist all operator actions to ts_audit_log table. Log: operator username, timestamp, action type, agent ID (if applicable), command, parameters, result status. Audit every: agent task, listener create/start/stop, operator connect/disconnect, login attempt (success and failure), loot access. Expose via REST GET /audit with filtering/pagination.
close_reason: Implemented structured operator audit logging with REST filtering and pagination.

title: Implement listener lifecycle management
description: In crates/teamserver: listener manager for creating, starting, stopping, and deleting listeners. ListenerManager struct holds active listener handles (tokio::task::JoinHandle). Methods: create(config), start(name), stop(name), delete(name), list(). Persist state to DB. Handle errors (port already in use, etc). Expose to WebSocket command handlers and REST API.
close_reason: Implemented persisted listener lifecycle management with REST and WebSocket integration

title: Fix operator-facing typos in process/injection/token callback messages
description:
Multiple user-visible strings in crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs contain typos:
- Line 398: 'Successful killed process: {pid}' → 'Successfully killed process: {pid}'
- Line 431: 'Successful injected shellcode' → 'Successfully injected shellcode'
- Line 477: 'Successful impersonated {user}' → 'Successfully impersonated {user}'
- Line 479: 'Failed to impersonat {user}' (missing 'e') → 'Failed to impersonate {user}'
These strings appear in the operator UI so correct grammar matters. The test assertions on lines 1517 and 1534 encode the typos and must be updated alongside the fix.

title: Testing Infrastructure
description: Full test suite: unit tests for common crate (protocol parsing, crypto, config), unit tests for teamserver core, integration tests that spin up a real teamserver with a mock Demon agent checking in, and E2E tests with mock agent plus mock operator client connecting. Tests run in CI.

title: Implement REST API framework and OpenAPI spec
description: In crates/teamserver: REST API infrastructure. Mount /api/v1/ routes on the Axum router. API key authentication middleware (keys configurable in profile). Define OpenAPI spec (using utoipa crate). Rate limiting. JSON error responses. Versioning strategy. This lays the groundwork for all REST endpoint implementations.
close_reason: Implemented the versioned REST API framework with API-key auth, rate limiting, OpenAPI docs, and JSON errors.

title: Implement WebSocket command handlers (listener management)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle operator commands over WebSocket for listener management. ListenerNew: validate config, create listener, persist to DB, broadcast ListenerNew event. ListenerStart/Stop: lifecycle management. ListenerDelete. All responses broadcast to all connected operators so multi-operator sessions stay in sync.

title: Implement REST endpoints: loot, audit log, and operators
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints for: GET /loot (list credentials/files/screenshots with filtering), GET /loot/:id (download specific loot item), GET /audit (operator activity log with pagination and filtering by operator/agent/time), GET /operators (list connected operators - Admin only), POST /operators (create new operator - Admin only).

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description: Create the Cargo workspace root Cargo.toml at the repo root. Set up the workspace members: crates/teamserver, crates/client, crates/common. Create placeholder lib.rs/main.rs for each crate. Update .gitignore for Rust build artifacts (target/). Set up rustfmt.toml and clippy.toml with project-wide lint settings. Rust edition 2024.

title: Implement agent checkin handler (DEMON_INIT)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle DEMON_INIT (command 99) - the initial agent registration. Parse agent metadata from checkin payload: hostname, username, domain, IPs, PID, PPID, process name, arch, OS version, elevation status, sleep/jitter config. Create AgentInfo, store in registry and DB, broadcast AgentNew event to all operators. Return success response to agent.

title: Implement TLS certificate generation
description: In crates/common: self-signed TLS certificate generation for the teamserver HTTPS listener and WebSocket server. Use rcgen crate to generate RSA/ECDSA certs at runtime (same approach as Havoc). Also support loading existing PEM certs from profile config. Integrate with rustls/tokio-rustls for use in Axum.

title: Client - Core Infrastructure
description: Core egui client binary: application setup, WebSocket client connection to teamserver (with TLS), operator authentication flow, JSON protocol handler and event processing loop. Foundation for all UI components.

title: Project Setup & Infrastructure
description: Initialize the Cargo workspace, set up directory structure, configure CI/CD pipeline, and establish the foundational project scaffolding for the Red Cell C2 Rust rewrite. This epic must be completed before any other development work begins.

title: Implement JSON WebSocket message types (operator protocol)
description: In crates/common: define all Rust structs (with serde Serialize/Deserialize) for the JSON-over-WebSocket operator protocol. Message structure: { Head: { Event, User, Timestamp }, Body: { SubEvent, Info } }. All event types: InitConnection, Login, ListenerNew, ListenerStart, ListenerStop, AgentNew, AgentUpdate, AgentTask, AgentResponse, Chat, BuildPayload, etc. Mirror the existing Havoc event system. Write unit tests for JSON round-tripping.

title: audit: OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove WebSocket path missing audit trail
description:
The WebSocket dispatch_operator_command function gained audit logging for ListenerNew and ListenerMark in 001245d, but OperatorMessage::ListenerRemove (crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:597-607) has no audit calls on either the success or failure branch.

When an operator removes a listener over the WebSocket protocol, no audit record is written. This is inconsistent with the REST DELETE which is also unaudited (see red-cell-c2-3kc).

Fix: add log_operator_action calls in both branches of the ListenerRemove match arm, using action label 'listener.delete', consistent with the pattern already used for ListenerNew/ListenerMark in the same function.

title: Implement REST endpoints: agents and listeners
description: In crates/teamserver: REST endpoints. Agents: GET /agents (list all), GET /agents/:id (details), DELETE /agents/:id (kill), POST /agents/:id/task (queue a job). Listeners: GET /listeners, POST /listeners (create), PUT /listeners/:name/start, PUT /listeners/:name/stop, DELETE /listeners/:name. Full RBAC checks on each.
close_reason: Implemented RBAC-protected REST endpoints for agents and listeners in crates/teamserver

title: Teamserver - Logging & Audit Trail
description: Structured logging throughout the teamserver using the tracing crate. Operator audit trail (who ran which command, on which agent, at what time). Loot/credential logging with search. Session activity recording. All logs persisted and queryable. New feature beyond Havoc.

title: Implement login dialog and authentication flow
description: In crates/client: login dialog UI (egui modal). Fields: server URL, username, password (masked). On submit: send InitConnection/Login WebSocket message with SHA3-256 hashed password. Handle auth success (transition to main UI) and failure (show error, stay on login). Save last-used server URL to local config file.

title: Set up structured logging with tracing crate
description: In crates/teamserver: configure the tracing crate for structured, leveled logging throughout the server. Set up tracing-subscriber with JSON formatter for production and pretty formatter for dev. Log levels configurable via RUST_LOG env var or profile. Instrument all major functions with tracing::instrument. Log to stdout and optionally to rolling file (tracing-appender).
close_reason: Implemented structured tracing with profile-driven formatting, optional rolling file logging, and instrumentation across core teamserver services

title: Unit tests for teamserver core
description: Unit tests for crates/teamserver core modules. Agent registry: concurrent insert/get/update, job queue operations. Event system: broadcast reaches all subscribers, lagging subscriber is dropped. Auth: correct password accepted, wrong rejected, SHA3-256 hash matches. RBAC: each role correctly allows/denies each permission. DB layer: test migrations run clean, CRUD operations work.

title: Implement DNS C2 listener
description:
In crates/teamserver: DNS-based C2 listener. Implement a DNS server that handles A/TXT/CNAME record queries as C2 communication channel. Profile-configurable domain and record types. Encode/decode Demon packets over DNS. This is a new channel beyond what Havoc currently provides. Lower priority - implement after HTTP and SMB are stable.

--- HANDOFF NOTES (2026-03-10, interrupted session) ---

Commit 7fa28af (feat(teamserver): partial DNS C2 listener implementation) contains a partial but functional foundation. Do NOT restart from scratch — build on this.

What was implemented in 7fa28af:
- DnsListenerConfig type added to crates/common/src/config.rs and domain.rs
- Core structs: DnsListenerState, DnsPendingUpload, DnsPendingResponse
- UDP socket scaffolding + listener lifecycle in spawn_dns_listener_runtime
- DNS packet handling: parse_dns_query, parse_dns_c2_query (upload/download queries)
- Base32hex encode/decode, chunk_response_to_b32hex
- Response builders: build_dns_txt_response, build_dns_refused_response
- Integration into the listener registry (listeners.rs ~line 1189)
- 2 passing integration tests: dns_listener_starts_and_responds_to_unknown_queries_with_refused, dns_listener_download_poll_returns_wait_when_no_response_queued

What remains — see open sub-issues:
- red-cell-c2-1am: parse_dns_query does not reject DNS response packets (QR bit unchecked)
- red-cell-c2-1c0: DnsListenerState::responses map leaks memory (completed downloads never removed)
- red-cell-c2-2g6: build_dns_txt_response hardcodes TXT QTYPE in question section
- red-cell-c2-2jw: DNS listener response map has no expiry (unbounded memory growth)
- red-cell-c2-uos: DNS upload assembler accepts untrusted chunk total from client (no upload session cap)

Work through the open sub-issues above, then run the full test suite before closing this issue.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on every push/PR: cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo fmt --check. Also add a release workflow that builds Linux x86_64 binaries for red-cell and red-cell-client. Cache cargo registry and target dir.
close_reason: Added GitHub Actions CI and release workflows with Cargo caching and Linux x86_64 artifacts

title: Set up Axum server binary and config loading
description: In crates/teamserver: main.rs entry point. Parse CLI args (clap): --profile <path>, --debug. Load and validate the HCL profile (from common crate). Initialize the Axum router with placeholders for all route groups (WebSocket /havoc/, HTTP agent listener, REST API /api/v1/). Bind to configured host:port with TLS (rustls). Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT.

title: Implement operator WebSocket handler and connection manager
description: In crates/teamserver: WebSocket endpoint at /havoc/. Upgrade HTTP connection to WebSocket using axum::extract::ws. Per-connection task: receive messages, parse JSON, dispatch to command handlers; subscribe to EventBus and forward broadcasts to client. Connection registry (track all active operator connections). Handle disconnects gracefully. Authentication check on first message before allowing further interaction.
close_reason: Implemented /havoc/ websocket handling with authenticated connection tracking and event forwarding.

title: Implement process list panel
description: In crates/client: process list UI. Tabular view: PID, PPID, name, arch, user, session. Refresh button. Actions: kill process, inject into process (opens injection dialog), migrate. Filter/search by name or PID. Highlight current agent process.

title: Teamserver - Payload Builder
description: Rust-based payload generation system for producing Demon agent binaries (EXE, DLL, shellcode). Wraps the existing MinGW/NASM compilation pipeline, applies profile-based configuration (sleep, jitter, injection settings, C2 listener endpoints), and delivers output to operators.

title: Implement loot logging and credential capture
description: In crates/teamserver: loot capture and management. Automatically detect and store: credentials in command output (pattern matching), downloaded files (via CALLBACK_FILE), screenshots (via COMMAND_SCREENSHOT). Persist to ts_loot table with metadata (type, agent, operator, timestamp, content/path). Broadcast LootNew event to operators.
close_reason: Implemented loot persistence for credentials, downloads, and screenshots with operator broadcasts

title: Implement command console (per-agent interactive shell)
description: In crates/client: interactive command console panel per agent. Input field with command history (up/down arrow). Output area with scrollback (formatted text, color for errors vs output). Tab completion for Demon commands. Display metadata header (agent hostname/user/pid). Multiple tabs or split view for multiple agents. Timestamps on output lines.

title: Embed Python runtime in client via PyO3
description: In crates/client: embed Python interpreter using PyO3. Load .py scripts from a configured scripts directory. Expose a client-side Python API: red_cell.register_command(name, callback), red_cell.on_agent_checkin(callback), red_cell.agent(id) returning an agent proxy object. Python runs in a dedicated thread with GIL. Error isolation (bad script doesn't crash client).

title: Implement job queue and COMMAND_GET_JOB handler
description: In crates/teamserver: handle COMMAND_GET_JOB (command 1) - agent polling for work. Dequeue all pending jobs from the agent's job queue. Serialize jobs into the Demon binary response format (command ID + params per job). If no jobs, return empty response. Handle COMMAND_CHECKIN (100) heartbeat: update agent LastCallIn timestamp in registry and DB, broadcast AgentUpdate event to operators.
close_reason: Implemented queued job draining, Demon job responses, and checkin heartbeat updates

title: DNS upload assembler accepts untrusted chunk total from client, no upload session cap
description:
In listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload (line 1473) accepts a u16 total value directly from the client DNS query and uses it as the expected chunk count, storing it in DnsPendingUpload::total without validation. An attacker can:
1. Send an upload query with total=65535 and seq=0 for a fabricated agent_id. This creates a DnsPendingUpload entry that will never complete (65535 chunks required) and stays in the uploads map for 120 seconds (DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS).
2. Repeat with thousands of distinct fake agent_ids without triggering the cleanup (retain is only called on the next incoming upload query). Between upload bursts, stale entries accumulate in the uploads Mutex<HashMap> without being pruned.
3. The total field is also not validated for consistency across chunks of the same agent_id — subsequent packets with a different total are silently ignored since the entry already exists, but adversarial early packets can set total to an unexpected value for a legitimate agent.

Fix: (a) Add a reasonable cap on total (e.g. 256 or 1024 chunks max), returning 'err' immediately if exceeded. (b) Add a periodic background cleanup task (e.g. tokio::spawn with interval) instead of relying on caller-triggered retain. (c) Add a max uploads map capacity check (e.g. 1000 concurrent sessions).

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload, lines 1473-1516 and handle_dns_packet line 1381.

title: Implement operator authentication
description: In crates/teamserver: operator login flow. Operators defined in HCL profile (username + plaintext password, hashed at load time with SHA3-256 to match Havoc). WebSocket handshake: client sends {User, Password_SHA3}. Server validates against profile. On success: issue session token (UUID or JWT), associate with WebSocket connection. Reject unknown users or wrong passwords. Write unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented SHA3-based operator login, session tokens, and WebSocket auth flow with tests

title: Implement token, injection, and shell commands
description: In crates/teamserver: handlers for COMMAND_TOKEN (steal/impersonate/revert), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL (inject DLL into remote process with shellcode), shell command execution, run (execute with output capture), and spawn (new process with token). Serialize jobs correctly per Demon binary protocol.

title: API key lookup uses non-constant-time comparison — timing oracle
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:127, the authenticate() method looks up API keys via BTreeMap::get(presented_key). BTreeMap string comparisons are NOT constant-time: they short-circuit on the first differing byte. An attacker with high-resolution network timing (e.g. co-located on the same LAN) could potentially enumerate valid key prefixes character by character. Fix: hash the presented key with HMAC-SHA256 (keyed with a per-process secret) before inserting into the BTreeMap, and hash the presented key the same way before lookup. Alternatively, store a SHA-256 (or bcrypt/argon2) digest of each configured key value in the map and compare digests. The subtle crate's ConstantTimeEq is unsuitable here since map lookup itself branches early; the pre-hashing approach is the idiomatic solution for bearer token comparison.
close_reason: Hashed configured API keys with per-process HMAC-SHA256 digests before lookup and added auth regression tests

title: Implement Python script manager UI
description: In crates/client: script manager panel. List loaded .py scripts with status (loaded/error). Load/unload/reload buttons. Script output console (stdout/stderr from scripts). File picker to load a new script. Error display for import failures. Status indicator showing how many commands each script registered.

title: Define shared error types and agent/listener structs
description: In crates/common: define the shared domain types used across teamserver and client. AgentInfo struct (all fields from TS_Agents DB schema: AgentID, Hostname, Username, DomainName, IPs, PID, ProcessName, Arch, Elevated, OSVersion, SleepDelay, Jitter, FirstCallIn, LastCallIn, etc). ListenerConfig enum (Http, Smb, External) with full config fields. OperatorInfo. Custom error types using thiserror.
close_reason: Implemented shared common domain types and thiserror-based validation errors

title: Implement session graph (node visualization)
description: In crates/client: egui node graph visualization of agent topology. Nodes represent agents, edges represent pivot relationships (SMB links). Drag-to-pan, scroll-to-zoom. Node color by status (active/dead). Click node to open agent console. Use egui_graphs crate or implement custom with egui Painter. Show teamserver as root node.

title: Implement pivot management (SMB links)
description: In crates/teamserver: manage agent pivot relationships. When an agent reports an SMB pivot link, store parent/child relationship in ts_links table and registry. Route jobs for child agents through parent agent's job queue (encapsulated in pivot packet format). Handle pivot chain teardown when parent agent dies.
close_reason: Implemented SMB pivot link persistence, routed child jobs through pivot packets, and added recursive link teardown on parent death.

title: Implement agent response handler (CALLBACK_*)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all agent callback response types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT (command stdout/result), CALLBACK_ERROR (error message), CALLBACK_FILE (file download chunk with offset/total). Match response to original request ID. Format output and broadcast AgentResponse event to operators. Handle multi-chunk file downloads (reassemble). Persist output to DB for history.

title: Implement file system commands
description: In crates/teamserver: file system command handlers. Upload (send file to agent in chunks), Download (receive file from agent via CALLBACK_FILE, reassemble, persist to loot), directory listing, file delete, file copy, directory navigation. Track download progress and broadcast to operators.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible filesystem task encoding, upload chunk staging, and download callback persistence/broadcasting

title: Teamserver - Core Infrastructure
description: Core teamserver infrastructure: Axum HTTP server setup, SQLite database layer (sqlx), in-memory agent registry, and event broadcast system. This is the backbone that all other teamserver components build upon.
